input.claim: Muizzu administration announces decision to establish a dedicated mental health hospital in the Greater Malé Area as its own funded initiative, while an inherited mental health hospital project in L. Gan with external financial assistance has stalled.
input.what_actually_happened: Article 36525 (2026-04-27) acknowledges that 'a mental health hospital project is currently underway in L. Gan with external financial assistance, progress has been slow.' Rather than accelerating the inherited project, the Muizzu administration announced a brand-new hospital in the Greater Malé Area 'funded by the Administration under a revised policy initiative.' This frames a new government-credit initiative while the pre-existing (pre-November 2023) externally-funded project — which the current government inherited — is being sidelined due to slow progress, a hallmark pattern of credit repositioning.

notes: Live verifier output for fact_check #49 (CREDIT THEFT). 3 evidence rows; distribution: {'confirms': 2, 'context': 1}. PINNED — relevance classifications on third-party news are subjective; hand-review before promoting.
